scope: operator-facing explanation boundary for local successor/comparator evidence
operator_summary: The local successor/comparator claim is registered but blocked.
These artifacts define what evidence would be needed before TideFS can make local OpenZFS, DRBD, ext4/XFS, or local-filesystem comparator wording.
Current evidence in this issue does not tell an operator that TideFS is a local filesystem successor, faster option, safer option, or production substitute.
operator_safe_statements: Local mounted filesystem evidence must name source ref, device/media, mount tier, workload, fault model, and measurement method.
Rows without a matching incumbent envelope remain evidence inventory, not operator guidance.
operator_unsafe_statements: TideFS is an OpenZFS, DRBD, ext4, XFS, or local-filesystem successor.
TideFS has proven better local performance than an incumbent filesystem.
A single local benchmark or source review is enough for production storage guidance.
Distributed, clustered, RDMA, or block-volume behavior follows from local mounted-userspace evidence.
required_future_evidence_before_operator: digest-matched mounted local artifacts for each cited workload
matched comparison envelope or a clear statement that no incumbent comparison is being made
claim-boundary review showing the registry status has moved through the normal claims gate
operator-facing wording review that keeps non-comparable cases visible
residual_risk: This explanation is a source-model artifact and does not run a mounted workload.
It is suitable for keeping operator wording bounded, not for admitting a local performance or reliability claim.
